topic: A person tying a Bowline knot with a thick rope, hands-only close-up.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: front view close-up of hands forming the bowline loop at the end of the thick rope.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: side-angle close-up of hands guiding the thick rope's working end up through the loop and around the standing part.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: top-down close-up of hands tightening the bowline to complete the knot.
parsed_generation.narration: The sequence follows a hands-only bowline: loop formation, guiding the working end through and around, then tightening the knot. Cinematic close-ups lock each step on the thick rope, creating a single fluid motion across eight seconds.

feedback:
The sequence captures hands-only knot-work on a thick rope with clear camera angles, but the timestamp blocks do not match the gold standard’s [00:00-00:03], [00:03-00:06], [00:06-00:08] structure. To improve, align the shots to the three canonical bowline steps: (1) form the loop and pass the working end up through it, (2) wrap around the standing part and back down through the original loop, (3) tighten by pulling both ends. Use standard terminology (rabbit hole, tree) and explicitly show feeding back down through the original loop for fidelity.

fixes: Explicitly depict the second step as feeding the end back down through the original loop and the final tightening.
